mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor demand and rising household income, which drives consumer spending and corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:October 2014 payroll level at 159,626 thousand persons
regime_hint: post-crisis expansion with steady labor recovery
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive payroll growth months do not produce significantly higher 20-day forward returns than negative payroll months (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate above 3.5% signals monetary tightening that raises discount rates and borrowing costs, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as the market reprices growth expectations downward.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle approaching rate liftoff
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 3.5%, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window in tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts above 1,200 thousand units combined with retail sales growth signals strong consumer demand and construction activity, which accelerates GDP growth and equity earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,307 thousand units in June 2014, RSAFS at 733,258 million dollars in July 2014
regime_hint: post-crisis housing recovery with sustained demand
failure_condition: Joint signal (HOUST > 1,200 AND RSAFS growth > 3% YoY) does not produce significantly higher 20-day returns than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC of interaction term < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index declining month-over-month signals weakening manufacturing activity and supply-side constraints that reduce corporate profit margins, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as the market reprices earnings expectations downward.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO months produce positive 5-day returns in > 50% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rising above 4.5% signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer confidence, which dampens spending and earnings growth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as the market reprices recession risk.
regime_hint: late-cycle labor market tightening with recession risk emerging
failure_condition: UNRATE > 4.5% periods produce positive 20-day returns in > 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerating above 2.5% year-over-year combined with elevated federal funds rate signals monetary policy tightening cycle that compresses equity valuations through higher discount rates and reduced real earnings growth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 324.368 in July 2014, FEDFUNDS at 3.88% in September 2014
regime_hint: stagflationary pressure with monetary tightening response
failure_condition: Joint trigger (inflation > 2.5% YoY AND FEDFUNDS > 3.5%) does not produce significantly negative 60-day returns compared to baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or interaction Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) accelerating above 2.5% annualized signals robust economic expansion that drives corporate earnings and equity valuations upward, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as growth expectations are revised higher.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 23,770.976 billion chained 2017 dollars in Q2 2014
failure_condition: Quarters with GDPC1 growth > 2.5% annualized do not produce significantly higher 60-day forward returns than slower-growth quarters (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth combined with declining unemployment rate signals accelerating supply capacity utilization and tight labor markets that compress profit margins through rising input costs, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as the market reprices margin compression risk.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 101.4279 in July 2014, UNRATE at 4.4% in July 2014
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with capacity constraints and wage pressure
failure_condition: Joint trigger (INDPRO growth > 0.3% MoM AND UNRATE < 4.5%) does not produce significantly negative 20-day returns compared to baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or interaction Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.
